topic: service temperature for light dry white wine

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A light-bodied dry white wine relies on freshness and delicate primary aromas.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Cool service emphasises acidity and freshness and limits the prominence of alcohol, while excessive cold can mute aroma.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: A range around 6-8 C is commonly suitable for simple light dry whites, followed by gradual warming in the glass.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: light dry whites are served cool to protect freshness, but not frozen.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
